name: Finn
summary: Writing so I can find out what I actually think.
long_summary: Finn's writing site. The central question: how symbols, language, and systems shape — and limit — our cognition and sense of self. The essays start from semiotics, continental philosophy, and the technical present, and try to pry apart concepts that look self-evident — ability, freedom, growth, knowledge, action — to see where their referents actually land. Written irregularly, slowly, and at length.
beliefs: Naming is compression. Every compression costs an experience.
Splicing and extending are two different operations. Most things called innovation belong to the first.
Empty signifiers bypass meaning and pull directly on emotion. Recognizing them is the prerequisite for immunity.
A subject is not declared into existence. It is trained, under real cost.
The line between invocation and understanding is the most important cognitive divide of this era.
